title: astephanus fallax
intro: Astephanus fallax, also known as the false holly, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Astephanus fallax include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Astephanus fallax are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
